issues.body:
The added '^[[:space:]]*' anchor on the R4 secret-literal grep (line 85) correctly eliminates a false positive: the old unanchored regex matched deploy/gcp/sparq-server.yaml:77 ('run.googleapis.com/secrets: sparq-auth-token:projects/PROJECT_NUMBER/secrets/sparq-auth-token'), a legitimate Secret Manager REFERENCE, causing the deploy-lint job to fail with a bogus 'possible plaintext credential' error. That fix is needed.

But by anchoring the KEYWORD itself to line-start (only optional whitespace may precede 'token|password|passwd|private_key'), the new pattern no longer matches any key with a prefix before the keyword. I verified empirically against sample manifests: the OLD regex flags all of
  github_token: "ghp_..."
  access_token: AKIA...EXAMPLE...
  db_password: "..."
  token: "..."
  password: "..."
while the NEW regex matches ONLY the bare 'token:' and 'password:' lines. github_token / access_token / db_password with an inline 24+ char literal now slip through undetected.

This is a gating (exit 1) R4 security control, and the dropped patterns ('*_token:', '*_password:', 'access_key:') are precisely the most common accidental-commit shapes the scan exists to catch. The coverage loss is undocumented and rides inside a PR otherwise presented as a mechanical SHA-bump, so it is easy to miss. Because a strictly-better fix exists that fixes the false positive WITHOUT the coverage loss, the current form should not merge as-is.
issues.fix_hint:
Anchor the LINE but still allow a key prefix before the keyword, e.g.:
  -E "^[[:space:]]*[A-Za-z0-9_.-]*(token|password|passwd|private[_-]?key)[A-Za-z0-9_.-]*[[:space:]]*:[[:space:]]*['\"]?[A-Za-z0-9+/=_-]{24,}['\"]?[[:space:]]*$"
I verified against the real deploy/ tree that this variant does NOT match the GCP Secret Manager reference at deploy/gcp/sparq-server.yaml:77 (the leading '[A-Za-z0-9_.-]*' run stops at the '/' in 'run.googleapis.com/secrets', so no keyword follows at the anchor) yet DOES flag github_token / access_token / db_password inline literals. Alternatively, keep the current anchor but split this functional security-control change out of the SHA-bump PR and document the coverage decision.
issues.title: R4 credential-scan regex over-narrows: line-start keyword anchor silently drops detection of prefixed keys (github_token, access_token, db_password)

verdict.summary: Two logically distinct changes ride in this PR. (1) actions/checkout SHA bumps from v4.2.2 (11bd719...) to the repo-canonical v7.0.0 pin (9c091bb...) across deploy-terraform-lint.yml (x2), promote-on-approval.yml, retriage.yml, triage-issue.yml, plus a comment-format normalization in deploy-lint.yml. Verified: all 121 checkout references now pin the single v7.0.0 SHA, zero v4.2.2 stragglers remain — this part is correct and complete. (2) NEW in this round: deploy-lint.yml's R4 plaintext-credential grep gains a '^[[:space:]]*' line-start anchor. Empirically this fixes a real false positive — the old unanchored regex matched the legitimate GCP Secret Manager reference at deploy/gcp/sparq-server.yaml:77 ('run.googleapis.com/secrets: sparq-auth-token:projects/...'), which explains the 'repair failing CI legs' commit. However, anchoring the KEYWORD (not just the line) to the start silently drops detection of prefixed credential keys: I confirmed by test that 'github_token:', 'access_token:', and 'db_password:' with inline 24+ char literals are matched by the OLD regex but MISSED by the NEW one. These are among the most common credential-leak key names, and this is a gating (exit 1) security control. A strictly-better one-line fix ('^[[:space:]]*[A-Za-z0-9_.-]*(token|password|passwd|private[_-]?key)...') fixes the false positive AND preserves that coverage (verified against the real deploy/ tree). The change is otherwise low-risk; requesting the regex be tightened before merge. No hostile instructions in the diff.
